  reasoning_backend: openai_sdk
  task_understanding: Run a parse-only extraction of the existing torsion-snapshot artifact bundle round_13_torsion_snapshots to produce a per-snapshot table of vertical-state summary values (no new calculations or CT-descriptor extraction).
  reasoning_summary: The requested operation (parse existing torsion snapshot bundle and report per-snapshot S1 energy and oscillator strength plus identify the first bright state per snapshot and a short trend summary) matches the registry action parse_snapshot_outputs. That action supports returning per-snapshot excitation energies, oscillator strengths, and state-ordering summaries. To ensure state-order switching (S1→S2 bright shifts) can be detected, request a modest state window covering the lowest several vertical states (1–5).
  capability_limit_note: parse_snapshot_outputs parses available snapshot vertical-state records but does not guarantee extraction of CT diagnostics (NTOs, Δμ, electron–hole separation) or other localization proxies. If those CT-specific observables are needed later, use extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle or inspect_raw_artifact_bundle (these are separate actions). This parsing action will not run any new calculations (perform_new_calculation=false) per the Planner instruction.
  expected_outputs: ["Per-snapshot table with columns: angle_deg, S1_energy_ev, S1_f, first_bright_state_index, first_bright_energy_ev, first_bright_f.", "Brief trend summary commenting on: whether S1 oscillator strength collapses (S1 becomes dark) at any angle, whether the brightest state at those angles shifts index (e.g., bright state moves to S2), and whether any state-index switching (ordering changes) is observed across angles.", "If any required fields are missing in the bundle (e.g., missing oscillator strengths or missing state indices), a clear note of missing coverage will be provided."]
  reasoning_parse_mode: reasoned_action_text
  reasoning_contract_mode: reasoned_action_text
  reasoning_contract_errors: []

- round=15 stage=reasoning status=end
  reasoning_backend: openai_sdk
  task_understanding: Planner requests a parse-only execution of existing torsion snapshot artifacts: run parse_snapshot_outputs on artifact_bundle_id="round_03_torsion_snapshots" (no new calculations) and return, for each snapshot angle, a table with angle_deg, S1_energy_ev, S1_f, first_bright_state_index, first_bright_energy_ev, first_bright_f, plus a short trend summary describing where S1 becomes dark, whether the bright state shifts to S2 at twisted angles, and any state-index switching.
  reasoning_summary: The requested operation matches the registry action parse_snapshot_outputs, which is designed to parse per-snapshot excitation energies, oscillator strengths, and state-ordering summaries from an existing artifact bundle without launching new calculations. The Planner explicitly forbids new calculations or CT-descriptor extraction; parse_snapshot_outputs supports parse-only reuse (perform_new_calculation=false, reuse_existing_artifacts_only=true). Therefore the task is representable exactly by a single parse_snapshot_outputs call with artifact_bundle_id set to "round_03_torsion_snapshots" and an appropriate state_window to capture S1 and nearby bright states.
  capability_limit_note: parse_snapshot_outputs returns per-snapshot excitation energies, oscillator strengths, and state-ordering summaries when those records exist in the bundle. It cannot synthesize CT diagnostics (NTO separation, Δμ, electron–hole distance/CT indices) if those observables are not present in the artifacts; the dedicated extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle action would be required for bounded CT-descriptor extraction. No new calculations will be performed per the Planner constraint.
  expected_outputs: ["A per-snapshot table (one row per recorded snapshot angle) with fields:", "angle_deg", "S1_energy_ev", "S1_f", "first_bright_state_index (index of the lowest-energy state with non-negligible oscillator strength)", "first_bright_energy_ev", "first_bright_f", "A brief trend summary (2–4 sentences) addressing:", "At which angles (if any) S1 oscillator strength collapses (S1 becomes dark).", "Whether the first bright state shifts to S2 (state-index switching from S1→S2 as bright) at twisted angles.", "Any observed state-index switching across the angular series.", "If required records are missing in the artifact bundle, a clear note describing which snapshots/observables were unavailable and which table entries could not be produced."]
  reasoning_parse_mode: reasoned_action_text
  reasoning_contract_mode: reasoned_action_text

- round=16 stage=reasoning status=start
  task_instruction: Execute ONLY one registry action. - round=16 stage=reasoning status=end
  reasoning_backend: openai_sdk
  task_understanding: The Planner requests a single microscopic registry action. Since both torsion scans have already been parsed into workspace records, the Planner instructs to run inspect_raw_artifact_bundle on one torsion snapshot bundle (no new calculations) to retrieve stored parsed_snapshot_records and produce a consolidated side-by-side cross-dihedral table and summary stats to support the TICT vs neutral-aromatic comparison.
  reasoning_summary: The registry exposes inspect_raw_artifact_bundle which can inspect a reusable artifact bundle and report available raw files and extractable observables. This action can be used (with requested_observable_scope) to retrieve stored parsed_snapshot_records from one bundle without launching calculations. It matches the Planner constraint of a single action and "no new calculations." CT-specific descriptor extraction remains unsupported by this action and must be reported if absent.
  capability_limit_note: inspect_raw_artifact_bundle reports raw-file coverage and extractable observable families but does not guarantee chemistry-specific CT descriptor extraction. If parsed_snapshot_records are absent from the inspected bundle, the worker cannot both inspect and parse the missing bundle in this single decision — a follow-up parse_snapshot_outputs action would be required.

### Round 1 | planner | planner_initial

- diagnosis: Task: infer the most likely AIE mechanism label (ICT/TICT/ESIPT/neutral aromatic/unknown) for the provided molecule using available prepared-structure context and initial computations. Top1 working hypothesis is TICT/RIM-like behavior because the molecule is a neutral, multi-aryl, partially rotatable system (rotatable_bond_count=4) where excited-state twisting/relaxation could be emissive-off in solution and become suppressed in aggregates; however, Top2 (neutral aromatic) remains plausible because the scaffold is also highly planar/rigid (planarity_proxy~0.91, many fused/aromatic rings) and may not require a distinct twisted CT minimum to explain AIE—simple restriction of intramolecular motions and reduced internal conversion could dominate. ESIPT is unlikely due to lack of OH/NH. Shared prepared structure is available (ready) with selected conformer and XYZ/SDF paths, enabling a bounded baseline S0 optimization + S1 vertical excitation to probe whether low-lying excitations show CT signatures or strong geometry-driven twisting propensity. Macro can reason from structural motifs, known AIE design patterns (silole/aryl rotors, thiophene-fused aromatics) and suggest discriminative observables; Microscopic can run exactly one low-cost baseline S0/S1 calculation on the provided prepared structure to extract excitation character proxies (oscillator strength, transition nature, frontier orbitals) but cannot by itself prove aggregation effects. First round should gather both: macro motif-based expectation + microscopic baseline excited-state descriptors to separate TICT/ICT vs neutral-aromatic RIM.

- action: macro_and_microscopic

- confidence: 0.47

- task_instruction: Run first-round Macro and one bounded baseline Microscopic S0/S1 calculation on the shared prepared structure to obtain initial evidence for CT/twisting vs neutral-aromatic behavior; do not attempt conformer/torsion sensitivity or multi-step follow-ups in round 1.

- agent_task_instructions: {"macro": "Assess macro-level structural evidence relevant to the current working hypothesis 'TICT'. Reuse the shared prepared structure context when available, summarize low-cost structural indicators only, and stay within current macro capability. Reuse the shared prepared structure context when available. Prioritize semi-empirical or otherwise low-cost bounded evidence collection, not heavy exhaustive geometry optimization. Report local microscopic results only, and do not attempt mechanism discrimination beyond current microscopic capability. Microscopic: in round 1 can run a single bounded baseline S0/S1 job on the provided prepared structure to extract excitation energies/oscillator strengths and qualitative CT vs LE indicators; it cannot model aggregation explicitly, cannot do multiple conformers/torsions in the first round, and should not make global mechanism judgments."}

### Round 1 | macro | run_macro

- agent_name: macro

- status: success

- task_received: Assess macro-level structural evidence relevant to the current working hypothesis 'TICT'. Reuse the shared prepared structure context when available, summarize low-cost structural indicators only, and stay within current macro capability. Hypothesis uncertainty to keep in mind: Top1 (TICT) is uncertain because structural proxies indicate both (a) presence of rotatable aryl ‘rotors’ that can enable twisted excited-state relaxation and (b) high overall planarity/rigidity that can favor a largely locally-excited neutral-aromatic emitter; baseline S1 character (CT vs LE) and evidence of strong donor–acceptor separation are needed to distinguish TICT from neutral aromatic.

- task_completion_status: contracted

- task_completion: Task was completed only in a capability-limited contracted form. The agent returned bounded macro evidence, but it could not complete unsupported parts of the Planner instruction: Any electronic-structure determination of S1 character (CT vs LE) or excited-state torsion driving force; Aggregate-state/packing effects or restriction-of-intramolecular-motion modeling; Global mechanism adjudication (confirming/refuting TICT as the dominant mechanism).

- task_understanding: Provide macro-level (single-molecule) structural evidence relevant to the working hypothesis TICT, using the shared prepared structure context when available. Summarize only low-cost topology/geometry-proxy indicators (rotors, donor–acceptor separation proxies, rigidity/planarity proxies) without making a global mechanism adjudication.

- reasoning_summary: Shared structure context is available for the neutral, closed-shell molecule (63 atoms) with multiple fused/aryl ring systems and heteroatoms (3). Low-cost indicators show a mixed picture for TICT: (i) presence of a small number of rotatable bonds (rotatable_bond_count=4; torsion_candidate_count=4) consistent with some aryl-aryl/aryl-core torsional freedom that could enable twisted conformations in S1; but (ii) high planarity/rigidity proxies (planarity_proxy=0.906, compactness_proxy=0.926) and multiple ring systems (aromatic_ring_count=6, ring_system_count=5) suggest a largely rigid/planar π-framework that can favor locally-excited character and reduce the likelihood/extent of large-amplitude twisting needed for classic TICT. Donor–acceptor partition proxy is moderate (0.5) with only 3 heteroatoms and no strong explicit donor/acceptor groups evident from the SMILES, so strong D–A separation (a hallmark that supports CT/TICT) is not strongly indicated by these proxies alone. Conformer dispersion proxy is high (1.0) with 10 conformers prepared, implying accessible conformational variability, but the planarity proxy suggests that variability may be limited to peripheral torsions rather than large decoupling of donor/acceptor fragments.
Capability limit note: This macro agent can only use provided preparation outputs and deterministic topology/geometry proxies; it cannot compute excited-state CT character, energy barriers to twisting, or aggregation/packing constraints that often decide TICT behavior in practice.

- execution_plan: Use deterministic_macro_structure_scan to execute the bounded macro workflow with focus areas: Rotor/torsion availability: rotatable_bond_count, torsion_candidate_count, aryl substituent topology, Rigidity/planarity: planarity_proxy, ring_system_count, aromatic_ring_count, compactness_proxy, Donor–acceptor separation proxy: donor_acceptor_partition_proxy, hetero_atom_count, presence/absence of strong D/A motifs in SMILES, Conformational accessibility: conformer_count and conformer_dispersion_proxy as a qualitative indicator. Planned local steps: [shared_context_load] Load the shared prepared structure context and reuse its descriptors. [focus_selection] Select the macro structural focus areas requested by the Planner instruction. [topology_analysis] Summarize rotor topology, ring systems, conjugation, and donor-acceptor layout. [geometry_proxy_analysis] Summarize planarity, torsion, compactness, and conformer-dispersion proxies.

- result_summary: The macro scan recorded aromatic_atom_count=36, hetero_atom_count=3, branch_point_count=13, rotatable_bond_count=4, planarity_proxy=0.905734, compactness_proxy=0.926017, and conformer_dispersion_proxy=1.0.
Key proxies: aromatic_atom_count=36, hetero_atom_count=3, branch_point_count=13, conjugation_proxy=10.406, flexibility_proxy=11.5, rotatable_bond_count=4, aromatic_ring_count=6, ring_system_count=5, donor_acceptor_partition_proxy=0.5, planarity_proxy=0.905734, compactness_proxy=0.926017, conformer_dispersion_proxy=1.0.

- remaining_local_uncertainty: Macro evidence alone cannot resolve excited-state relaxation behavior or external consistency; unresolved local gap: Macro evidence is limited to single-molecule low-cost structural and geometry proxies, so it still cannot resolve excited-state relaxation behavior, aggregation-state packing, or external consistency.

Failure policy: If the new baseline calculation fails, report the failure with error diagnostics and preserve raw Amesp output for inspection. - execution_plan: Execute the real bounded Amesp route "baseline_bundle" as follows: 
Expected outputs from this bounded run: low-cost aTB S0 geometry optimization, vertical excited-state manifold characterization
If Amesp fails, return the available partial artifacts and local uncertainty only.

- result_summary: Amesp route 'baseline_bundle' finished with final_energy_hartree=-77.7593930176, homo_lumo_gap_ev=2.9798524, rmsd_from_prepared_structure_angstrom=1.276549, and 63 Mulliken charges. Bounded S1 vertical excitation returned first_excitation_energy_ev=3.3865 and first_oscillator_strength=0.8674 across 5 states. First bright state energy=3.3865 with pattern=lowest_state_is_bright. ### Round 1 | planner | planner_diagnosis

- diagnosis: Top1=neutral aromatic (0.44) vs Top2=TICT (0.33). Latest evidence weakens the previous Top1 (TICT): macro proxies indicate a largely rigid/planar polyaromatic framework with only limited rotor count and weak D–A separation; microscopic baseline shows the lowest excitation is already strongly bright (S1 f≈0.867), which is more typical of locally-excited aromatic emission than a CT/TICT-dark precursor. This correspondingly strengthens the neutral-aromatic challenger. ICT remains a secondary candidate because heteroaromatic sulfurs and a moderate S0 dipole could contribute CT mixing, but lack of clear D–A pattern keeps it behind. Remaining key discriminator between neutral aromatic vs TICT: whether accessible torsions produce a markedly different excited-state character (CT increase and oscillator strength quenching) at twisted geometries (i.e., a dark TICT minimum). Verifier supplementation is still missing; closure justification is still missing. Best next action is a targeted microscopic torsion-snapshot sweep on the key rotatable bonds to see if twisting drives CT-like state formation and brightness loss, which directly discriminates TICT from neutral-aromatic LE behavior.

- action: microscopic

- confidence: 0.44

- task_instruction: Microscopic agent: run a bounded torsion-snapshot study to discriminate neutral aromatic vs TICT. Use the existing prepared/S0-optimized structure. Select the top 2 most relevant torsion candidates among the 4 rotatable bonds that most likely decouple aromatic subunits from the core. For each selected dihedral, run snapshots at angle offsets [-90, -45, 0, +45, +90] degrees (or closest supported), and for each snapshot compute vertical excitations for states 1–5 with oscillator strengths. Additionally, extract any available CT/charge-separation descriptors from the existing baseline bundle and from the snapshot outputs (e.g., delta charge distribution, electron-hole separation proxies if supported by extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle/parse_snapshot_outputs). Deliverables: (1) f(S1) vs torsion angle, (2) whether the lowest bright state switches to higher state upon twisting, (3) any monotonic increase in CT descriptors upon twisting. Keep it low-cost; no excited-state geometry optimization.

- agent_task_instructions: {"microscopic": "Run `run_torsion_snapshots` for 2 key dihedrals (choose the ones most likely to twist an aryl substituent relative to the central silacyclic/conjugated core). Use angle offsets [-90, -45, 0, +45, +90]. For each snapshot, compute vertical excitations (states 1–5) and report excitation energies and oscillator strengths. Then run `parse_snapshot_outputs` to summarize trends and run `extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle` on the existing `round_01_baseline_bundle` (and on snapshots if supported) to obtain qualitative CT vs LE indicators. Return only local results (tables/trends) without global mechanism judgment."}

- hypothesis_uncertainty_note: Primary uncertainty is whether torsional motion actually generates a distinct dark/CT-like excited state (TICT) versus merely modulating vibronic nonradiative decay in an LE aromatic emitter (neutral aromatic). Current vertical-only single-conformer baseline cannot confirm an S1 TICT minimum or state reordering upon twisting.

- final_hypothesis_rationale: null

- capability_assessment: Macro provided reliable topology/rigidity proxies but cannot assess excited-state character. Microscopic baseline provided bright-state ordering and basic S0 properties, but did not yet extract CT descriptors or torsion dependence; a torsion-snapshot sweep is within supported microscopic capabilities and is the most discriminative next internal step before external verification.

Microscopic baseline provided bright-state ordering and basic S0 properties, but did not yet extract CT descriptors or torsion dependence; a torsion-snapshot sweep is within supported microscopic capabilities and is the most discriminative next internal step before external verification.", "stagnation_assessment": "No stagnation: this is the first evidence-driven update and it changed the top-1 hypothesis; next step is a targeted discriminative microscopic task.", "contraction_reason": "Macro and microscopic were capability-contracted away from heavy excited-state optimization/DFT; evidence collected is still useful for discrimination via low-cost proxies and vertical excitations.", "information_gain_assessment": "Medium: baseline vertical brightness strongly suggests LE character, but does not eliminate TICT without testing torsion dependence; a torsion snapshot sweep is likely to provide high discriminative value at low cost.", "gap_trend": "narrowing (from generic structural proxies to a specific torsion/CT discriminator).", "runner_up_hypothesis": "TICT", "runner_up_confidence": 0.33, "hypothesis_reweight_explanation": {"neutral aromatic": "Upweighted because high planarity/rigidity proxies plus an already very bright lowest vertical excitation are more consistent with LE aromatic emission modulated by RIM than with a CT/TICT-dark state.", "TICT": "Downweighted because limited D–A separation proxies and bright S1 reduce classic TICT plausibility, though some rotatable torsions keep it viable.", "ICT": "Slightly downweighted; moderate dipole/heteroaromatics could allow ICT mixing but lack of strong D–A motif and bright S1 keep it secondary.", "ESIPT": "Remains very low due to absence of proton donor/acceptor functionality.", "unknown": "Slightly upweighted to reflect remaining unmeasured discriminator (torsion-dependent CT/state reordering) and possible mixed mechanisms."}, "decision_pair": ["neutral aromatic", "TICT"], "decision_gate_status": "needs_pairwise_discriminative_task", "verifier_supplement_target_pair": "neutral aromatic__vs__TICT", "verifier_supplement_status": "missing", "verifier_information_gain": "none", "verifier_evidence_relation": "no_new_info", "verifier_supplement_summary": null, "closure_justification_target_pair": "neutral aromatic__vs__TICT", "closure_justification_status": "collecting", "closure_justification_evidence_source": "internal", "closure_justification_basis": "new_targeted_task", "closure_justification_summary": "Plan: obtain torsion-dependent vertical excitation/oscillator-strength trends and CT descriptors from low-cost microscopic snapshots; ### Round 1 | memory | update_working_memory

- round_id: 1

- action_taken: macro, microscopic

- main_gap: Direct evidence of torsion-driven CT formation and oscillator-strength quenching (signature of TICT) versus persistence of a bright LE state under twisting (supports neutral aromatic).

- next_action: microscopic

- evidence_summary: macro: Task completion: Task was completed only in a capability-limited contracted form. The agent returned bounded macro evidence, but it could not complete unsupported parts of the Planner instruction: Any electronic-structure determination of S1 character (CT vs LE) or excited-state torsion driving force; Aggregate-state/packing effects or restriction-of-intramolecular-motion modeling; Global mechanism adjudication (confirming/refuting TICT as the dominant mechanism).
Task understanding: Interpret the Planner instruction as a macro-level structural analysis task for the current working hypothesis "TICT": Assess macro-level structural evidence relevant to the current working hypothesis 'TICT'. Reuse the shared prepared structure context when available, summarize low-cost structural indicators only, and stay within current macro capability. Hypothesis uncertainty to keep in mind: Top1 (TICT) is uncertain because structural proxies indicate both (a) presence of rotatable aryl ‘rotors’ that can enable twisted excited-state relaxation and (b) high overall planarity/rigidity that can favor a largely locally-excited neutral-aromatic emitter; baseline S1 character (CT vs LE) and evidence of strong donor–acceptor separation are needed to distinguish TICT from neutral aromatic.
Shared 3D structure context is available and reused from /datasets/workspace/mas_aie/var/runtime/tools/shared_structure/6b78cd78323e/prepared_structure.xyz.
This agent should only summarize low-cost structural evidence and should not make a global mechanism judgment or recommend a system-level next action.
Reasoning summary: Shared structure context is available for the neutral, closed-shell molecule (63 atoms) with multiple fused/aryl ring systems and heteroatoms (3). Low-cost indicators show a mixed picture for TICT: (i) presence of a small number of rotatable bonds (rotatable_bond_count=4; torsion_candidate_count=4) consistent with some aryl-aryl/aryl-core torsional freedom that could enable twisted conformations in S1; but (ii) high planarity/rigidity proxies (planarity_proxy=0.906, compactness_proxy=0.926) and multiple ring systems (aromatic_ring_count=6, ring_system_count=5) suggest a largely rigid/planar π-framework that can favor locally-excited character and reduce the likelihood/extent of large-amplitude twisting needed for classic TICT. Donor–acceptor partition proxy is moderate (0.5) with only 3 heteroatoms and no strong explicit donor/acceptor groups evident from the SMILES, so strong D–A separation (a hallmark that supports CT/TICT) is not strongly indicated by these proxies alone. Conformer dispersion proxy is high (1.0) with 10 conformers prepared, implying accessible conformational variability, but the planarity proxy suggests that variability may be limited to peripheral torsions rather than large decoupling of donor/acceptor fragments.
Capability limit note: This macro agent can only use provided preparation outputs and deterministic topology/geometry proxies; it cannot compute excited-state CT character, energy barriers to twisting, or aggregation/packing constraints that often decide TICT behavior in practice.
Execution plan: Use deterministic_macro_structure_scan to execute the bounded macro workflow with focus areas: Rotor/torsion availability: rotatable_bond_count, torsion_candidate_count, aryl substituent topology, Rigidity/planarity: planarity_proxy, ring_system_count, aromatic_ring_count, compactness_proxy, Donor–acceptor separation proxy: donor_acceptor_partition_proxy, hetero_atom_count, presence/absence of strong D/A motifs in SMILES, Conformational accessibility: conformer_count and conformer_dispersion_proxy as a qualitative indicator. Planned local steps: [shared_context_load] Load the shared prepared structure context and reuse its descriptors. [focus_selection] Select the macro structural focus areas requested by the Planner instruction. [topology_analysis] Summarize rotor topology, ring systems, conjugation, and donor-acceptor layout. [geometry_proxy_analysis] Summarize planarity, torsion, compactness, and conformer-dispersion proxies.
Result summary: The macro scan recorded aromatic_atom_count=36, hetero_atom_count=3, branch_point_count=13, rotatable_bond_count=4, planarity_proxy=0.905734, compactness_proxy=0.926017, and conformer_dispersion_proxy=1.0.
Key proxies: aromatic_atom_count=36, hetero_atom_count=3, branch_point_count=13, conjugation_proxy=10.406, flexibility_proxy=11.5, rotatable_bond_count=4, aromatic_ring_count=6, ring_system_count=5, donor_acceptor_partition_proxy=0.5, planarity_proxy=0.905734, compactness_proxy=0.926017, conformer_dispersion_proxy=1.0.

### Round 2 | microscopic | run_microscopic

- agent_name: microscopic

- status: failed

- task_received: Run `run_torsion_snapshots` for 2 key dihedrals (choose the ones most likely to twist an aryl substituent relative to the central silacyclic/conjugated core). Use angle offsets [-90, -45, 0, +45, +90]. For each snapshot, compute vertical excitations (states 1–5) and report excitation energies and oscillator strengths. Then run `parse_snapshot_outputs` to summarize trends and run `extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle` on the existing `round_01_baseline_bundle` (and on snapshots if supported) to obtain qualitative CT vs LE indicators. Return only local results (tables/trends) without global mechanism judgment.

- task_completion_status: failed

- task_completion: Task could not be completed. The Planner requested a microscopic action that is not represented in the current Amesp action registry: Sequential execution of multiple distinct execution actions in a single decision: run_torsion_snapshots followed by parse_snapshot_outputs followed by extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle; Running extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle on two different bundles (round_01_baseline_bundle and the newly generated torsion_snapshots) within the same single decision.

- task_understanding: Planner requests a bounded torsion-snapshot study targeting two key rotatable dihedrals (chosen by relevance), with snapshots at angles [-90, -45, 0, +45, +90], computing vertical excitations (states 1–5) and oscillator strengths for each snapshot. After that, the Planner asks to (a) parse snapshot outputs to summarize excitation-energy / oscillator-strength trends and (b) run CT-descriptor extraction on the existing round_01_baseline_bundle and on the generated torsion snapshots. All results are to be returned locally (tables/trends) without a global mechanism judgment.

- reasoning_summary: Local reasoning summary: The Amesp registry exposes three separate execution actions that together implement the requested workflow: run_torsion_snapshots (produce snapshots + vertical excitations), parse_snapshot_outputs (parse/summary of existing snapshot artifacts), and extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle (extract CT surrogates from existing bundles, including baseline and torsion_snapshots). However, the local operational rule for this worker requires selecting at most one execution action per decision. Because the Planner's instruction requires a sequence of multiple distinct execution actions, it cannot be represented exactly by a single registry-backed action in one local decision.
Capability limit note: Limitation: this worker decision must choose at most one execution action. The registry supports each required step as separate execution actions, but the requested sequential execution (torsion snapshots → parse snapshots → extract CT descriptors on two bundles) is not representable as a single execution_action in this local decision. Therefore the full Planner instruction is unsupported here. Any needed decomposition must be issued as multiple separate worker actions (one execution action per decision).
Failure policy: This decision returns "unsupported" rather than attempting a partial or proxied substitution. To succeed, the Planner should split the workflow into separate requests so that each worker decision can select one execution action at a time (e.g., a run_torsion_snapshots decision; then a parse_snapshot_outputs decision referencing the resulting torsion_snapshots bundle; and extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle decisions for baseline_bundle and torsion_snapshots). Any attempt to bundle multiple execution actions into one decision will be rejected by the local execution rules.

- execution_plan: Execute the real bounded Amesp route "unsupported" as follows: No execution steps were run because the Planner explicitly requested registry-unsupported microscopic task(s): Sequential execution of multiple distinct execution actions in a single decision: run_torsion_snapshots followed by parse_snapshot_outputs followed by extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle; Running extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle on two different bundles (round_01_baseline_bundle and the newly generated torsion_snapshots) within the same single decision.
Expected outputs from this bounded run: No outputs were produced because tool execution did not start.
If Amesp fails, return the available partial artifacts and local uncertainty only.

- result_summary: Microscopic execution did not start because the Planner explicitly requested a registry-unsupported local task. Blocked request(s): Sequential execution of multiple distinct execution actions in a single decision: run_torsion_snapshots followed by parse_snapshot_outputs followed by extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle; Running extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle on two different bundles (round_01_baseline_bundle and the newly generated torsion_snapshots) within the same single decision. No substitute microscopic action was executed.

- remaining_local_uncertainty: Microscopic local uncertainty after this Amesp run: the current Amesp registry does not expose the requested microscopic action, so no new local evidence was collected in this round

- generated_artifacts: {}

### Round 2 | planner | planner_diagnosis

- diagnosis: Top1=neutral aromatic (0.46) vs Top2=TICT (0.31). Latest usable evidence (macro rigidity/planarity proxies + microscopic baseline vertical excitations with a very bright S1) strengthens neutral aromatic relative to TICT, but does not eliminate TICT because torsion-driven state reordering/CT formation has not been tested. The latest attempted microscopic discriminative task failed for procedural/registry reasons (requested multi-step workflow in one decision), so no new torsion/CT evidence was added; thus the top1 remains supported but not closed. Key remaining discriminator: does twisting along the most relevant dihedrals cause (i) oscillator-strength collapse and/or lowest-bright-state switching to higher index (TICT-like) with increased CT descriptors, versus (ii) persistence of a bright lowest state with little CT growth (neutral aromatic). Verifier supplementation is still missing; closure justification is still collecting (blocked by the need to run the pairwise discriminative torsion-snapshot task as separate supported steps). The best next action is a single supported microscopic execution action to generate torsion-snapshot vertical excitations, enabling subsequent parsing/CT-descriptor extraction in later steps.

- action: microscopic

- confidence: 0.46

- task_instruction: Microscopic agent (single action only): run `run_torsion_snapshots` (do not parse or extract CT descriptors in this same decision). Use the existing S0-optimized geometry from `round_01_baseline_bundle` if supported; otherwise use the shared prepared structure. Select 2 most relevant dihedrals among the 4 torsion candidates that most decouple an aryl/heteroaryl unit from the central conjugated core. For each selected dihedral, generate snapshots at angle offsets [-90, -45, 0, +45, +90] degrees (or closest supported). For each snapshot compute vertical excitations for states 1–5 with oscillator strengths. Deliverables: the produced torsion_snapshots bundle_id plus a compact table of (dihedral_id, angle, S1 energy, S1 f, first_bright_state_index, first_bright_state_energy, first_bright_state_f).

- agent_task_instructions: {"microscopic": "Execute ONLY `run_torsion_snapshots` for 2 key dihedrals (chosen to most decouple peripheral aryl/heteroaryl from the core). Angles: [-90,-45,0,+45,+90] (or closest supported). States: 1–5 with oscillator strengths. Return torsion snapshot bundle_id and a compact per-snapshot summary table: angle vs (S1 energy,f; first_bright_state_index, energy,f). Do NOT run `parse_snapshot_outputs` or `extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle` in this same action."}

- hypothesis_uncertainty_note: Primary uncertainty is whether accessible torsions produce a distinct CT-like/dark twisted configuration (TICT) or whether the excited state remains largely locally excited and bright across torsion (neutral aromatic). The attempted torsion/CT follow-up failed due to registry sequencing constraints, leaving this discriminator unmeasured.

- final_hypothesis_rationale: null

- capability_assessment: Macro evidence (rigidity/planarity and limited D–A proxy) and baseline vertical excitations (bright S1) support LE/neutral aromatic, but decisive discrimination requires torsion-dependent vertical excitation trends. Microscopic tooling can do this via `run_torsion_snapshots`, but the workflow must be decomposed into single-action steps (run snapshots first; parse/extract CT descriptors in subsequent separate actions).

- reasoning_summary: Local reasoning summary: The requested action matches the run_torsion_snapshots execution route exposed in the registry. Angle grid and state window map directly to angle_offsets_deg and state_window. Dihedral selection will be guided by a pre-execution discovery (list_rotatable_dihedrals) using peripheral-inclusive and bond-type preferences to pick the two dihedrals that best decouple peripheral aryl/heteroaryl fragments from the core. Snapshot_count is set to cover both dihedrals × 5 angles (10 total bounded snapshots) so the bundle contains the requested five-angle sweep for each of the two chosen targets. The run will perform new calculations and will not perform excited-state relaxation or any CT-extraction/parse actions in this decision.
Capability limit note: The registry does not expose an explicit plural dihedral_ids parameter for selecting multiple named dihedrals in one call. To meet the "two dihedrals" requirement within a single run_torsion_snapshots action, this decision relies on discovery-guided automatic selection (list_rotatable_dihedrals) plus a bounded snapshot_count (10) to allocate 5 angles per selected dihedral. If an explicit dihedral_id list is required later, a subsequent decision must supply those IDs.
Failure policy: If discovery returns fewer than two suitable dihedrals, the worker should (1) select the available high-relevance dihedrals up to two and proceed, and (2) return an explicit note in the run output indicating the shortfall and which dihedral IDs were used. If the backend cannot honor the exact angle offsets, the worker should use the closest supported offsets and report the applied angles.

### Round 3 | planner | planner_diagnosis

- diagnosis: Top1=TICT (0.62) vs Top2=neutral aromatic (0.23). Latest evidence strengthens TICT: torsion snapshots show oscillator-strength collapse of the lowest state (S1 f drops to ~0.016–0.10 across angles) and state reordering where the first bright state appears at higher index (pattern=lowest_state_is_dark_then_bright; example snapshot S1 f=0.032, S2 f=0.118). This weakens the neutral-aromatic challenger because a purely LE aromatic emitter would be expected to keep a bright lowest transition more robustly under twisting. ICT remains plausible but secondary because we still lack explicit CT descriptors (electron–hole separation / charge transfer metrics) to confirm CT character versus other symmetry/overlap effects. Key remaining discriminator between TICT vs neutral aromatic: confirm that the darkened torsion-induced lowest state is CT-like (via CT descriptors) and verify whether the effect generalizes across more than one key dihedral. Verifier supplementation is still missing; closure justification is collecting (we now have torsion-dependent brightness/state-order evidence, but need parsing + CT descriptors for decisiveness). Next action is best now: parse the torsion snapshot bundle to extract per-angle trends and explicit first-bright-state indices/oscillator strengths cleanly for closure-ready comparison against the baseline.

- action: microscopic

- confidence: 0.62

- task_instruction: Microscopic agent (single action): run `parse_snapshot_outputs` on artifact_bundle_id `round_03_torsion_snapshots`. Deliverables: a compact table by snapshot (angle) reporting (S1 energy, S1 f, first_bright_state_index, first_bright_state_energy, first_bright_state_f) and an overall summary of (i) whether lowest_state_is_dark_then_bright holds across angles, (ii) min/median/max S1 f, and (iii) any state-index switching events.

- agent_task_instructions: {"microscopic": "Execute ONLY `parse_snapshot_outputs` for bundle_id=`round_03_torsion_snapshots`. Return a per-snapshot table: angle_deg, S1_energy_ev, S1_f, first_bright_state_index, first_bright_energy_ev, first_bright_f; plus a brief trend summary (does S1 remain dark across angles? any state reordering points?). Do NOT run CT-descriptor extraction in this action."}

- hypothesis_uncertainty_note: Although torsion snapshots show strong bright-to-dark behavior consistent with TICT, CT character has not yet been directly confirmed (no CT-descriptor extraction), and only one explicit dihedral_id was resolved in the snapshot run; both factors leave residual ambiguity between TICT and alternative dark-state origins.

- final_hypothesis_rationale: null

- capability_assessment: Microscopic capability successfully produced torsion-dependent vertical excitations and revealed state reordering/oscillator-strength collapse, which is highly discriminative for TICT vs neutral aromatic. However, the workflow still requires separate single-action steps to (a) parse snapshot outputs and (b) extract CT descriptors from baseline and torsion bundles; multi-step chaining in one decision is not allowed.

Reasoning summary: Local reasoning summary: The requested action matches the run_torsion_snapshots execution route exposed in the registry. Angle grid and state window map directly to angle_offsets_deg and state_window. Dihedral selection will be guided by a pre-execution discovery (list_rotatable_dihedrals) using peripheral-inclusive and bond-type preferences to pick the two dihedrals that best decouple peripheral aryl/heteroaryl fragments from the core. Snapshot_count is set to cover both dihedrals × 5 angles (10 total bounded snapshots) so the bundle contains the requested five-angle sweep for each of the two chosen targets. The run will perform new calculations and will not perform excited-state relaxation or any CT-extraction/parse actions in this decision.
Capability limit note: The registry does not expose an explicit plural dihedral_ids parameter for selecting multiple named dihedrals in one call. To meet the "two dihedrals" requirement within a single run_torsion_snapshots action, this decision relies on discovery-guided automatic selection (list_rotatable_dihedrals) plus a bounded snapshot_count (10) to allocate 5 angles per selected dihedral. If an explicit dihedral_id list is required later, a subsequent decision must supply those IDs.
Failure policy: If discovery returns fewer than two suitable dihedrals, the worker should (1) select the available high-relevance dihedrals up to two and proceed, and (2) return an explicit note in the run output indicating the shortfall and which dihedral IDs were used. If the backend cannot honor the exact angle offsets, the worker should use the closest supported offsets and report the applied angles.
Execution plan: Execute the real bounded Amesp route "torsion_snapshot_follow_up" as follows: 
Expected outputs from this bounded run: vertical excited-state manifold characterization, torsion-sensitivity summary
If Amesp fails, return the available partial artifacts and local uncertainty only.

Do not attempt new calculations in this action.

### Round 4 | planner | planner_diagnosis

- diagnosis: Top1=TICT (0.66) vs Top2=neutral aromatic (0.18). The latest parsed torsion-snapshot evidence strengthens TICT: S1 oscillator strength collapses at more twisted angles (e.g., -90°, +45°, +90°: f≈0.0158–0.032) and the first bright state can shift to S2 (lowest_state_is_dark_then_bright), indicating torsion-enabled access to a dark state. This weakens the neutral-aromatic challenger, which would more typically retain a bright lowest state across torsion. ICT remains a secondary alternative because a dark state could arise from CT character in general; however, CT descriptors are not yet extracted, so the key remaining discriminator is whether the torsion-accessed dark state is genuinely charge-transfer separated (supporting TICT/CT) versus a non-CT dark LE-like state. Verifier supplementation is still missing. Closure justification is collecting: we now have angle-wise oscillator-strength/state-ordering evidence, but still lack CT descriptors and broader dihedral coverage. Best next action is a single microscopic CT-descriptor extraction on the torsion snapshot bundle to directly test CT character of the dark state and separate TICT from neutral aromatic/LE-dark alternatives.

- action: microscopic

- confidence: 0.66

- task_instruction: Microscopic agent (single action only): run `extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle` on artifact_bundle_id=`round_03_torsion_snapshots`. Deliverables: per-snapshot CT/charge-separation descriptors (or an explicit availability report if some are not supported), aligned with snapshot_label/angle. Summarize whether CT metrics increase at twisted angles where S1 becomes dark and whether the lowest dark state exhibits stronger CT character than the first bright state. Do not run any new torsion scans or parsing in this action.

- agent_task_instructions: {"microscopic": "Execute ONLY `extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle` for bundle_id=`round_03_torsion_snapshots`. Return a compact per-snapshot table keyed by (snapshot_label, target_angle_deg) with any available CT descriptors (e.g., electron–hole separation proxy, charge-transfer amount/proxy, dipole change proxy if available). Also report descriptor availability/limitations explicitly. Do NOT run `run_torsion_snapshots` or `parse_snapshot_outputs` in this action."}

- hypothesis_uncertainty_note: Main uncertainty is mechanistic: torsion clearly modulates brightness and state ordering, but CT character of the torsion-accessed dark state has not been confirmed because CT proxies were not available in parse output. A CT-descriptor extraction is required to distinguish true TICT from a torsion-induced non-CT dark state (or generic ICT mixing).

- final_hypothesis_rationale: null

- capability_assessment: Microscopic tooling successfully produced and parsed torsion-dependent excitation energies/oscillator strengths and state-ordering patterns. However, CT/localization information was not available from parsing and must be obtained via the dedicated `extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle` action (one action per decision).

### Round 5 | planner | planner_diagnosis

- diagnosis: Top1=TICT (0.64) vs Top2=neutral aromatic (0.19). Latest evidence leaves Top1 largely strengthened: torsion snapshots/parsed tables show oscillator-strength collapse of the lowest state at twisted angles (e.g., -90°: S1 f≈0.0158) and bright-state shifting to S2 (lowest_state_is_dark_then_bright), which is hard to reconcile with a purely neutral-aromatic LE emitter remaining bright across torsion. However, the attempted CT-descriptor extraction confirms that key CT metrics (electron–hole separation / CT proxy / dipole-change proxy) are not available from stored artifacts, so CT character of the dark state remains unconfirmed. This keeps the key discriminator unresolved: is the torsion-accessed dark state specifically CT-separated (TICT) versus a non-CT dark state within an otherwise aromatic system (or generic ICT mixing). Verifier supplementation is still missing. Closure justification is still collecting because the remaining closure item (CT-character confirmation) is blocked by missing extractable descriptors. ### Round 6 | verifier | run_verifier

- agent_name: verifier

- status: success

- task_received: Verifier: For molecule MGISAKGHBIWFDS-UHFFFAOYSA-N (SMILES C[Si]1(C)C(c2ccc3sccc3c2)=C(c2ccccc2)C(c2ccccc2)=C1c1ccc2ccsc2c1), distinguish TICT vs neutral aromatic given internal evidence: (i) very bright baseline S1 at FC point (f~0.87) but (ii) torsion snapshots along dihedral [13,20,21,22] show S1 becomes dark (f~0.016–0.10) and first bright state shifts to S2 at twisted angles. Provide external precedents/criteria indicating whether such torsion-induced darkening + state reordering in similar silole/thiophene-aryl systems is typically attributed to TICT (CT-separated) vs non-CT symmetry/overlap loss. Identify key experimental signatures (solvatochromism, polarity-dependent emission, Lippert-Mataga trends, dual emission, time-resolved behavior) and minimal computational diagnostics (NTO separation, Δμ, electron–hole distance, attachment/detachment densities) that would discriminate TICT vs neutral aromatic here, and any warning signs that challenge TICT assignment in this scaffold.

- task_completion_status: completed

- task_completion: Task completed successfully by retrieving raw verifier evidence for Planner review.

- task_understanding: Interpret the Planner instruction as an external supervision retrieval task for the current working hypothesis "TICT": Verifier: For molecule MGISAKGHBIWFDS-UHFFFAOYSA-N (SMILES C[Si]1(C)C(c2ccc3sccc3c2)=C(c2ccccc2)C(c2ccccc2)=C1c1ccc2ccsc2c1), distinguish TICT vs neutral aromatic given internal evidence: (i) very bright baseline S1 at FC point (f~0.87) but (ii) torsion snapshots along dihedral [13,20,21,22] show S1 becomes dark (f~0.016–0.10) and first bright state shifts to S2 at twisted angles. Provide external precedents/criteria indicating whether such torsion-induced darkening + state reordering in similar silole/thiophene-aryl systems is typically attributed to TICT (CT-separated) vs non-CT symmetry/overlap loss. Identify key experimental signatures (solvatochromism, polarity-dependent emission, Lippert-Mataga trends, dual emission, time-resolved behavior) and minimal computational diagnostics (NTO separation, Δμ, electron–hole distance, attachment/detachment densities) that would discriminate TICT vs neutral aromatic here, and any warning signs that challenge TICT assignment in this scaffold.
This agent should only retrieve and summarize relevant raw evidence cards and should not decide whether the hypothesis should be kept, switched, strengthened, weakened, supported, or conflicted.

- reasoning_summary: Reasoning summary was not provided.

- result_summary: The verifier retrieved 4 evidence card(s) covering these topics: tict, diagnostic_criteria, solvatochromism, emission, experimental_validation, silole, torsion, emission_mechanism, charge_transfer, computational_chemistry, excited_states, descriptors, methodology, limitations.

- remaining_local_uncertainty: Verifier evidence cards summarize external supervision but do not replace Planner-level synthesis; unresolved local gap: the evidence cards still need Planner-level synthesis before any mechanism decision.

- generated_artifacts: {}

### Round 6 | planner | planner_reweight_or_finalize

- diagnosis: Top1=TICT (0.61) vs Top2=neutral aromatic (0.20). Verifier evidence is high information gain but largely generic/close-family: it supports that torsion-induced darkening + state reordering is consistent with TICT in silole/aryl systems, yet simultaneously challenges using that signature alone as decisive because non-CT conjugation breaking can produce similar patterns. Verifier adds concrete discriminators (solvatochromism/dual emission/Δμ; computational NTO separation/e–h distance) that directly map to our blocked internal gap. Closure justification remains blocked on missing CT-character confirmation; verifier supplementation for the pair is sufficient, but it does not unlock decisive closure by itself. Not ready to finalize; best next step is a targeted internal task that attempts to access CT diagnostics via raw-artifact inspection or an alternative compute route, otherwise best-available closure may be required later with explicit uncertainty.

- action: microscopic

- confidence: 0.61

- task_instruction: Microscopic agent (single action only): run `inspect_raw_artifact_bundle` on artifact_bundle_id=`round_03_torsion_snapshots` AND report whether any raw outputs contain attachment/detachment densities, NTOs, excited-state dipoles, or any electron–hole/CT metrics not captured by `extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle`. If present, extract/quote the relevant numerical proxies (e.g., Δμ, any e–h distance proxy) for at least the most twisted snapshot(s) (±90°) and the 0° snapshot. If absent, explicitly confirm absence and list what observables are available per snapshot. Do not run new calculations.

- agent_task_instructions: {"microscopic": "Execute ONLY `inspect_raw_artifact_bundle` for bundle_id=`round_03_torsion_snapshots`. Check raw files for any CT-relevant outputs not surfaced by the extractor: NTO/attachment-detachment, excited-state dipole (or Δμ), electron–hole distance/separation, any CT indices. If found, return a compact table for snapshots at -90°, 0°, +90° (by snapshot_label/angle) with the extracted CT metrics; if not found, explicitly state that these observables are not present in the raw artifacts and list available observables."}

- hypothesis_uncertainty_note: Current uncertainty is not about torsion sensitivity (strongly established) but about CT character: verifier explicitly warns that state reordering/darkening can occur without CT, so labeling the dark twisted state as TICT (vs non-CT twisted dark state or more general ICT mixing) remains non-decisive without CT diagnostics (NTO separation, Δμ, e–h distance) or solvent-dependent experimental signatures.

- final_hypothesis_rationale: null

- capability_assessment: Verifier supplementation provided concrete external discriminators and a close-family note for silole torsion darkening, but could not supply compound-specific CT confirmation. Internally, microscopic vertical excitations and torsion snapshots are strong; the limiting capability is the absence of extractable CT descriptors in current snapshot outputs, motivating a raw-artifact inspection or an alternate compute configuration that outputs CT diagnostics.

### Round 6 | memory | update_working_memory

- main_gap: Obtain any direct CT-character evidence for the torsion-accessed dark state (e.g., NTO hole/particle separation, Δμ, electron–hole distance; or solvent-polarity emission diagnostics) to distinguish true TICT from non-CT torsional darkening / neutral aromatic and to separate TICT from generic ICT mixing.

- next_action: microscopic

- evidence_summary: verifier: Task completion: Task completed successfully by retrieving raw verifier evidence for Planner review.
Task understanding: Interpret the Planner instruction as an external supervision retrieval task for the current working hypothesis "TICT": Verifier: For molecule MGISAKGHBIWFDS-UHFFFAOYSA-N (SMILES C[Si]1(C)C(c2ccc3sccc3c2)=C(c2ccccc2)C(c2ccccc2)=C1c1ccc2ccsc2c1), distinguish TICT vs neutral aromatic given internal evidence: (i) very bright baseline S1 at FC point (f~0.87) but (ii) torsion snapshots along dihedral [13,20,21,22] show S1 becomes dark (f~0.016–0.10) and first bright state shifts to S2 at twisted angles. Provide external precedents/criteria indicating whether such torsion-induced darkening + state reordering in similar silole/thiophene-aryl systems is typically attributed to TICT (CT-separated) vs non-CT symmetry/overlap loss. Identify key experimental signatures (solvatochromism, polarity-dependent emission, Lippert-Mataga trends, dual emission, time-resolved behavior) and minimal computational diagnostics (NTO separation, Δμ, electron–hole distance, attachment/detachment densities) that would discriminate TICT vs neutral aromatic here, and any warning signs that challenge TICT assignment in this scaffold.
This agent should only retrieve and summarize relevant raw evidence cards and should not decide whether the hypothesis should be kept, switched, strengthened, weakened, supported, or conflicted.
Execution plan: Use verifier_evidence_lookup to retrieve raw evidence cards for the current hypothesis across these query groups: exact_identity, champion_family, challenger_family, pairwise_discriminator. Summarize the retrieved topics and report only local verifier findings.
Result summary: The verifier retrieved 4 evidence card(s) covering these topics: tict, diagnostic_criteria, solvatochromism, emission, experimental_validation, silole, torsion, emission_mechanism, charge_transfer, computational_chemistry, excited_states, descriptors, methodology, limitations.
Remaining local uncertainty: Verifier evidence cards summarize external supervision but do not replace Planner-level synthesis; unresolved local gap: the evidence cards still need Planner-level synthesis before any mechanism decision.

This agent may only return local electronic-structure evidence and must not make a global mechanism judgment or recommend the next system action.

- reasoning_summary: Local reasoning summary: The registry exposes inspect_raw_artifact_bundle which is the intended parse-only action for reporting raw artifact file coverage and extractable observable families. This action accepts a requested_observable_scope so I can request the specific CT-relevant families named by the Planner. The action must run without launching new calculations (perform_new_calculation=false) and can target the explicit artifact_bundle_id provided.
Capability limit note: inspect_raw_artifact_bundle reports raw-file inventories and which observable families the worker can extract from those files, but the registry warns it does not guarantee complete chemistry-specific descriptor extraction. If raw files contain explicit NTO/attachment–detachment outputs, excited-state dipoles, Δμ, or electron–hole separation metrics, the action will surface them; if those outputs are not present in the raw artifacts, the action must report their absence and list which observables are available instead.
Failure policy: - If the bundle ID is missing or not found, return a concise unsupported/failure note referencing the missing artifact_bundle_id.
- If the bundle exists but none of the requested CT observables are present, return the explicit absence statement plus the available-observables list (no inferred CT descriptors).
- If some but not all requested CT observable families are present, extract and tabulate the available metrics for the requested snapshot angles and explicitly list which CT observables remain unavailable.

- execution_plan: Execute the real bounded Amesp route "artifact_parse_only" as follows: 
Expected outputs from this bounded run: vertical excited-state manifold characterization, dipole summary, torsion-sensitivity summary
If Amesp fails, return the available partial artifacts and local uncertainty only.

### Round 7 | planner | planner_diagnosis

- diagnosis: Top1=TICT (0.52) vs Top2=neutral aromatic (0.23). Latest internal evidence (torsion snapshots + parsed outputs) continues to support a torsion-accessible dark lowest state (S1 f drops to ~0.016–0.10 and first bright transition shifts to S2 at twisted angles), which strengthens TICT relative to a torsion-insensitive neutral-aromatic LE picture. However, the latest microscopic raw-artifact inspection confirms CT-specific observables are absent (no NTOs, attachment/detachment, excited-state dipoles/Δμ, e–h separation, or CT indices), which weakens how decisively we can label the dark state as CT-driven TICT versus non-CT conjugation breaking; verifier evidence is sufficient but mixed and explicitly warns state reordering alone is insufficient. The key remaining discriminator between TICT and neutral aromatic is direct CT character of the twisted dark state (e.g., NTO hole/particle separation, Δμ, solvent-polarity signatures, or transient-absorption evidence for charge-separated species). Closure justification for TICT__vs__neutral aromatic remains blocked because the decisive CT discriminator is missing. Best next action is to pursue a new internal microscopic computation/configuration that can output CT/NTO diagnostics (since inspection confirms current artifacts cannot provide them).

- action: microscopic

- confidence: 0.52

- task_instruction: Microscopic agent (single action only): run `run_baseline_bundle` (new calculation) using settings/output options (if available) that explicitly request CT-relevant excited-state diagnostics for states 1–5: NTOs or attachment/detachment densities (hole/particle), excited-state dipole (or Δμ), and any electron–hole separation/CT indices. If the registry cannot request these observables, explicitly report that limitation and return any closest-available surrogate outputs that improve CT assessment. Goal: enable direct CT-character discrimination of the dark/twisted state mechanism (TICT vs non-CT conjugation breaking).

- agent_task_instructions: {"microscopic": "Execute ONLY one registry action. Prefer `run_baseline_bundle` with any available flags/options that output CT diagnostics (NTOs/attachment-detachment, excited-state dipoles/Δμ, e–h separation/CT indices) for states 1–5. If such outputs are not configurable in the registry, run the closest supported action that increases CT observability and clearly report which CT observables remain impossible to obtain."}

- hypothesis_uncertainty_note: Torsion-induced darkening/state reordering is robustly established, but CT character cannot be confirmed: both extractor and raw-artifact inspection show CT-relevant diagnostics (NTO/e–h/Δμ/CT indices) are absent. Verifier confirms that without CT evidence, TICT vs non-CT torsional darkening remains ambiguous.

- final_hypothesis_rationale: null

- capability_assessment: Microscopic tools successfully established torsion-dependent oscillator-strength collapse and state reordering, but current artifact formats do not include CT diagnostics and therefore cannot confirm CT character. Verifier supplementation is high-gain and sufficient but mixed; it cannot replace missing compound-specific CT metrics.

Reasoning summary: Local reasoning summary: The registry exposes inspect_raw_artifact_bundle which is the intended parse-only action for reporting raw artifact file coverage and extractable observable families. This action accepts a requested_observable_scope so I can request the specific CT-relevant families named by the Planner. The action must run without launching new calculations (perform_new_calculation=false) and can target the explicit artifact_bundle_id provided.
Capability limit note: inspect_raw_artifact_bundle reports raw-file inventories and which observable families the worker can extract from those files, but the registry warns it does not guarantee complete chemistry-specific descriptor extraction. If raw files contain explicit NTO/attachment–detachment outputs, excited-state dipoles, Δμ, or electron–hole separation metrics, the action will surface them; if those outputs are not present in the raw artifacts, the action must report their absence and list which observables are available instead.
Failure policy: - If the bundle ID is missing or not found, return a concise unsupported/failure note referencing the missing artifact_bundle_id.
- If the bundle exists but none of the requested CT observables are present, return the explicit absence statement plus the available-observables list (no inferred CT descriptors).
- If some but not all requested CT observable families are present, extract and tabulate the available metrics for the requested snapshot angles and explicitly list which CT observables remain unavailable.

Expected outputs from this bounded run: vertical excited-state manifold characterization, dipole summary, torsion-sensitivity summary
If Amesp fails, return the available partial artifacts and local uncertainty only.

Prefer `run_baseline_bundle` with any available flags/options that output CT diagnostics (NTOs/attachment-detachment, excited-state dipoles/Δμ, e–h separation/CT indices) for states 1–5. If such outputs are not configurable in the registry, run the closest supported action that increases CT observability and clearly report which CT observables remain impossible to obtain.
Requested local focus: low-cost aTB S0 geometry optimization, vertical excited-state manifold characterization, dipole summary

The registry does not expose parameters to request NTOs, attachment/detachment densities, excited-state dipoles/Δμ, or electron–hole separation/CT indices from a new baseline calculation, so those CT-specific observables are explicitly reported as unavailable via this action.

Executed capability: run_baseline_bundle
Performed new calculations: true

Unmet constraints: No unmet constraints have been recorded yet.

Capability boundary: Current microscopic capability is Amesp low-cost multi-route execution with protocolized capabilities: run_baseline_bundle, run_conformer_bundle, run_torsion_snapshots, parse_snapshot_outputs, extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle, and inspect_raw_artifact_bundle. unsupported_excited_state_relaxation is a fail-fast unsupported capability and does not execute.
Structure handling note: Reuse the shared prepared 3D structure context that is already available for this case at /datasets/workspace/mas_aie/var/runtime/tools/shared_structure/6b78cd78323e/prepared_structure.xyz.
Unsupported local requests that will not be executed in this run: NTOs (natural transition orbitals) and attachment/detachment densities; explicit per-excited-state dipoles or Δμ (excited-minus-ground dipole); electron–hole separation metrics (e–h distance) and CT indices
This agent may only return local electronic-structure evidence and must not make a global mechanism judgment or recommend the next system action.

- reasoning_summary: Local reasoning summary: The run_baseline_bundle action directly provides a low-cost aTB S0 optimization plus vertical excited-state manifold and S0 dipole (matching the requested deliverables) and accepts a state_window parameter to target states 1–5. The registry does not expose flags/parameters to request NTOs, attachment/detachment densities, per-state excited-state dipoles/Δμ, electron–hole separation metrics, or CT-index outputs from a freshly launched baseline calculation. Therefore the closest supported single action is run_baseline_bundle with state_window=[1,2,3,4,5]; any remaining CT-specific observables must be reported as unsupported by this registry action.
Capability limit note: Supported by run_baseline_bundle: S0 optimized geometry, S0 dipole, S0 Mulliken charges (where available), vertical excited-state energies and oscillator strengths, and state-ordering for the requested states. Not supported by run_baseline_bundle (no exposed parameters to request these): NTO hole/particle orbitals or their attachment/detachment densities; explicit per-excited-state dipoles or Δμ (excited minus ground dipole); electron–hole separation/e–h distance metrics or CT indices. The bundle-extraction actions (extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle / inspect_raw_artifact_bundle) can inspect existing artifacts for CT surrogates without new calculations, but do not enable requesting those CT diagnostics from a new run via exposed flags.
Failure policy: If the run_baseline_bundle execution fails to launch or complete (e.g., resource or backend error), return a structured failure report including the error cause and state. If new-calculation execution is impossible, consider a follow-up parse-only inspection of existing artifacts (using extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle or inspect_raw_artifact_bundle) outside this single-action decision, but do not execute additional actions in this step.

- execution_plan: Execute the real bounded Amesp route "baseline_bundle" as follows: 
Expected outputs from this bounded run: low-cost aTB S0 geometry optimization, vertical excited-state manifold characterization, dipole summary
If Amesp fails, return the available partial artifacts and local uncertainty only.

### Round 8 | planner | planner_diagnosis

- diagnosis: Top1=TICT (0.50) vs Top2=neutral aromatic (0.26). Latest evidence leaves Top1 unresolved/partially weakened: while torsion snapshots strongly support access to a twisted dark state (oscillator-strength collapse and bright state shifting to S2), the newest microscopic follow-up confirms CT-specific diagnostics are not available in raw artifacts and cannot be requested via run_baseline_bundle; verifier also warns state reordering alone is insufficient to claim CT/TICT. This slightly strengthens the Top2 challenger (neutral aromatic/non-CT conjugation-breaking) because the main counter-hypothesis can reproduce the same torsion-darkening signature without CT. An additional candidate affected is ICT: it remains plausible as a general CT-mixing explanation but cannot be distinguished from TICT without CT observables. Key remaining discriminator between TICT and neutral aromatic is direct evidence of charge separation in the twisted dark state (e.g., NTO hole/particle separation, Δμ, e–h distance/CT index) or experimental CT signatures (strong solvatochromism/dual emission/transient absorption of charge-separated species). Verifier supplementation is sufficient (high IG, mixed), but closure justification is blocked because decisive CT observables are missing and currently unproducible with the available microscopic registry outputs. Best next action is to seek additional external/metadata evidence (verifier) about whether this exact molecule (InChIKey) has reported solvatochromism/dual emission/TA signatures, since internal routes cannot currently generate CT diagnostics.

- action: verifier

- confidence: 0.5

- task_instruction: Verifier (TICT vs neutral aromatic): try to find any compound-specific photophysics for MGISAKGHBIWFDS-UHFFFAOYSA-N / C34H26S2Si / silole-aryl-thiophene system, focusing on whether emission shows (a) strong positive solvatochromism, (b) dual emission, (c) polarity-dependent quantum yield decrease, (d) transient absorption evidence for charge-separated species, or (e) reported excited-state dipole/CT assignment. Also look for close analogs of this exact silole substitution pattern with explicit assignment of torsion-darkening as CT vs non-CT conjugation breaking. Return citations/quotes and any quantitative thresholds reported.

- agent_task_instructions: {"verifier": "Verifier (TICT vs neutral aromatic): try to find any compound-specific photophysics for MGISAKGHBIWFDS-UHFFFAOYSA-N / C34H26S2Si / silole-aryl-thiophene system, focusing on whether emission shows (a) strong positive solvatochromism, (b) dual emission, (c) polarity-dependent quantum yield decrease, (d) transient absorption evidence for charge-separated species, or (e) reported excited-state dipole/CT assignment. Also look for close analogs of this exact silole substitution pattern with explicit assignment of torsion-darkening as CT vs non-CT conjugation breaking. Return citations/quotes and any quantitative thresholds reported."}

- final_hypothesis_rationale: null

- capability_assessment: Microscopic tools successfully established torsion-dependent oscillator-strength collapse/state reordering but cannot produce or recover CT observables (NTO/Δμ/e–h distance/CT indices). Verifier has provided generic and close-family discriminators and warnings, but compound-specific CT evidence is still missing.

- main_gap: Direct CT-character evidence for the torsion-accessed dark state (NTO separation, Δμ, electron–hole distance/CT indices, or experimental solvatochromism/dual emission/transient-absorption evidence of charge separation) to distinguish TICT from non-CT torsional darkening and to separate TICT from generic ICT.

Unsupported parts were: NTOs (natural transition orbitals) and attachment/detachment densities; explicit per-excited-state dipoles or Δμ (excited-minus-ground dipole); electron–hole separation metrics (e–h distance) and CT indices.

Prefer `run_baseline_bundle` with any available flags/options that output CT diagnostics (NTOs/attachment-detachment, excited-state dipoles/Δμ, e–h separation/CT indices) for states 1–5. Requested Amesp route summary: Run the low-cost aTB baseline bundle targeted to states 1–5 to deliver the requested S0 optimization, vertical manifold, and ground-state dipole. The registry does not expose parameters to request NTOs, attachment/detachment densities, excited-state dipoles/Δμ, or electron–hole separation/CT indices from a new baseline calculation, so those CT-specific observables are explicitly reported as unavailable via this action.

Capability boundary: Current microscopic capability is Amesp low-cost multi-route execution with protocolized capabilities: run_baseline_bundle, run_conformer_bundle, run_torsion_snapshots, parse_snapshot_outputs, extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle, and inspect_raw_artifact_bundle. unsupported_excited_state_relaxation is a fail-fast unsupported capability and does not execute.
Structure handling note: Reuse the shared prepared 3D structure context that is already available for this case at /datasets/workspace/mas_aie/var/runtime/tools/shared_structure/6b78cd78323e/prepared_structure.xyz.
Unsupported local requests that will not be executed in this run: NTOs (natural transition orbitals) and attachment/detachment densities; explicit per-excited-state dipoles or Δμ (excited-minus-ground dipole); electron–hole separation metrics (e–h distance) and CT indices
This agent may only return local electronic-structure evidence and must not make a global mechanism judgment or recommend the next system action.
Reasoning summary: Local reasoning summary: The run_baseline_bundle action directly provides a low-cost aTB S0 optimization plus vertical excited-state manifold and S0 dipole (matching the requested deliverables) and accepts a state_window parameter to target states 1–5. The registry does not expose flags/parameters to request NTOs, attachment/detachment densities, per-state excited-state dipoles/Δμ, electron–hole separation metrics, or CT-index outputs from a freshly launched baseline calculation. Therefore the closest supported single action is run_baseline_bundle with state_window=[1,2,3,4,5]; any remaining CT-specific observables must be reported as unsupported by this registry action.
Capability limit note: Supported by run_baseline_bundle: S0 optimized geometry, S0 dipole, S0 Mulliken charges (where available), vertical excited-state energies and oscillator strengths, and state-ordering for the requested states. Not supported by run_baseline_bundle (no exposed parameters to request these): NTO hole/particle orbitals or their attachment/detachment densities; explicit per-excited-state dipoles or Δμ (excited minus ground dipole); electron–hole separation/e–h distance metrics or CT indices. The bundle-extraction actions (extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle / inspect_raw_artifact_bundle) can inspect existing artifacts for CT surrogates without new calculations, but do not enable requesting those CT diagnostics from a new run via exposed flags.
Failure policy: If the run_baseline_bundle execution fails to launch or complete (e.g., resource or backend error), return a structured failure report including the error cause and state. If new-calculation execution is impossible, consider a follow-up parse-only inspection of existing artifacts (using extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle or inspect_raw_artifact_bundle) outside this single-action decision, but do not execute additional actions in this step.
Execution plan: Execute the real bounded Amesp route "baseline_bundle" as follows: 
Expected outputs from this bounded run: low-cost aTB S0 geometry optimization, vertical excited-state manifold characterization, dipole summary
If Amesp fails, return the available partial artifacts and local uncertainty only.

Return citations/quotes and any quantitative thresholds reported.

### Round 9 | planner | planner_reweight_or_finalize

- diagnosis: Top1=TICT (0.47) vs Top2=neutral aromatic (0.27). Verifier evidence is high information gain but generic/close-family and mixed: it provides decisive discriminator criteria for true TICT (strong solvatochromism/dual emission/large excited-state dipole/NTO separation/TA charge-separated signatures) while explicitly warning that torsion-induced darkening and state reordering alone can arise from non-CT conjugation breaking; it also notes close-family silole cases with minimal CT/solvatochromism. This challenges making a decisive TICT call from the existing internal torsion-darkening evidence. Closure justification remains blocked because CT-defining observables are absent from internal artifacts and not requestable via current registry actions. Not ready to finalize; next step must seek compound-specific external evidence or a new compute capability that outputs CT diagnostics.

- action: verifier

- confidence: 0.47

- task_instruction: Verifier (compound-specific, TICT vs neutral aromatic): locate any experimental/computational photophysics for MGISAKGHBIWFDS-UHFFFAOYSA-N / C34H26S2Si / canonical SMILES C[Si]1(C)C(c2ccc3sccc3c2)=C(c2ccccc2)C(c2ccccc2)=C1c1ccc2ccsc2c1, or the closest substitution-pattern analogs. Extract evidence bearing directly on CT vs non-CT mechanisms: (1) solvatochromism magnitude (report nm shift across solvent polarity), (2) dual emission presence/absence and assignments, (3) Lippert–Mataga slope or excited-state dipole estimates (if reported), (4) time-resolved/transient-absorption evidence for charge-separated species, (5) any reported computations (NTOs/attachment–detachment/e–h separation/CT indices) assigning LE vs CT for planar vs twisted conformations. Provide citations and quoted quantitative values; if no direct hits, report the closest analogs and explain similarity.

Verifier capability can supply generic criteria and close-family precedents but may not find compound-specific data.

- diagnosis: Top1=TICT (0.44) vs Top2=neutral aromatic (0.30). Verifier evidence is high information gain but generic/close-family and mixed: it provides discriminative criteria for confirming true TICT (e.g., strong solvatochromism/dual emission/large excited-state dipole or NTO separation/TA charge separation) and simultaneously emphasizes that torsion-induced darkening + state reordering is not CT-specific and can arise from non-CT conjugation breaking. This challenges making a decisive TICT call based on internal torsion snapshots alone. Closure justification for TICT vs neutral aromatic remains blocked because CT observables (NTO/Δμ/e–h separation/CT indices) are confirmed absent from internal artifacts and not requestable via current microscopic registry actions, and compound-specific literature evidence was not found. Not ready to finalize; best next step is an additional verifier supplement attempt focused strictly on closest substitution-pattern analogs or any reported solvent-series/time-resolved data for this exact InChIKey.

Internal evidence establishes a torsion-accessible dark state but lacks CT diagnostics, so closure remains blocked; the case is not ready for decisive or best-available finalization under current rules.

- reasoning_summary: Local reasoning summary: The requested operation maps directly to the registry action run_torsion_snapshots and may include the allowed discovery action list_rotatable_dihedrals to select an alternate high-relevance dihedral. Required parameters (angle_offsets_deg, state_window, exclude_dihedral_ids, min_relevance, include_peripheral, preferred_bond_types, snapshot_count, etc.) are available in the registry. The task forbids targeting dih_13_20_21_22, so exclude_dihedral_ids will be set and allow_fallback left false to ensure an explicit alternate candidate is selected or an explicit failure is reported.
Capability limit note: This action will produce snapshot vertical-state energies and oscillator strengths (the run_torsion_snapshots default deliverables). It cannot produce CT-specific diagnostics (NTOs, Δμ, electron–hole separation/CT indices) or perform excited-state relaxations—those observables are outside the current registry capabilities and will not be produced by this action.
Failure policy: If rotatable-dihedral discovery returns no alternate high-relevance dihedral aside from the excluded dih_13_20_21_22, the run will not fall back to the excluded dihedral (allow_fallback=false) and will return an explicit failure/report indicating no acceptable dihedral candidate was found. Any other execution errors will be surfaced as a run failure with diagnostic notes from the discovery and snapshot steps.
